id: network analysis labs/noxious (htb)/example of llmnr poisoning
title: Example of LLMNR poisoning

breadcrumbs.id: network analysis labs/noxious (htb)/example of llmnr poisoning
breadcrumbs.title: Example of LLMNR poisoning

overview: 1. Victim tries to access a share - A user types in \\fileserver\share or an application references that UNC path. - If DNS can’t resolve fileserver, Windows falls back to LLMNR. 2. Victim broadcasts query

study.overview: 1. Victim tries to access a share - A user types in \\fileserver\share or an application references that UNC path. - If DNS can’t resolve fileserver, Windows falls back to LLMNR. 2. Victim broadcasts query
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
